quotes.text: The scarlet color of the beast is the same color as the ribbon that was tied around the neck of the scapegoat on the Day of Atonement; the ribbon represented the sins of Israel. In the same way, the scarlet of the beast seems to represent sin (Isa. 1:18).
quotes.source: Understanding the Book of Revelation [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co.
quotes.attr: Donald W. Parry and Jay A. Parry, Understanding the Book of Revelation [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co., 1998], 221

quotes.text: And the woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet colour. Purple clothing symbolizes royalty (Judg. 8:26), and scarlet clothing symbolizes wealth and nobility (2 Sam. 1:24; Prov. 31:21; Lam. 4:5; Dan. 5:7; Matt. 27:28-29). The woman's clothing indicates her position of wealth and power in the world.
quotes.source: Understanding the Book of Revelation [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co.
quotes.attr: Donald W. Parry and Jay A. Parry, Understanding the Book of Revelation [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co., 1998], 221

notes: already been done before the Roman Catholic Church came into existence. The Gnostics, the Judaizers, and even Judaism itself had so corrupted the scriptures beforehand that it was the Roman Catholic Church that said, 'My Heavens to Betsy, let's get things tied down before everything's changed.' We should be grateful to the Roman Catholic Church to have received the Bible in as sound a condition as it is today. Oh, of course, it's correct that down through time Monks with their bald heads had to copy all the different scriptures, and marginal references were put here and there and they often made errors in copying, but the intentional corrupting of the materials of gospel scriptures was not done by the Roman Catholic Church. It was done a long time before the Roman Catholic Church came into existence. "Well, you say, 'I know, but it was the Roman Catholic Church that persecuted the Saints of God, wasn't it? So the Roman Catholic Church must then be the Great and Abominable Church.' May I submit to you that it was when the Roman Catholic Church came into existence that the persecution of the Saints ceased. Prior to that time, through Jewish efforts, through Rome, through Gnostics, through apostates, but more especially through the Roman Empire, the persecution of the Christians came about, which caused the blood of martyrs to be sucked up by the goddesses and gods of Rome. It was when the Roman Catholic Church came into existence that toleration began to come to the Saints of God, the accommodation process had taken place by now so that in time there was no difference between church and empire, and peace came to all Christians. "'Ah,' but you say, 'John told us for sure that he was talking about the Pope. Don't you know that the number 666 is referring to the Pope? Therefore, now we have proof that he was talking about the Roman Catholic Church.' I would guess at this point that probably forty-five per cent or more of the Missionary IP Books of all the missionaries in front of me have somewhere in the IP Book either under 'A' for apostasy or 'P' for Pope or 'C' for Catholic or something like this, a page; and on the page would be listed three words, but in vertical shape-- VICARIVS FILII DEI, the 'vicar of God.' By counting numerical equivalent to each of those letters, and using the Latin form of all the letters, you'll see that the V equals 5, and I equals one, the C equals 100, and an A happens to equal 0, (so we'll have to let that go), and R--we'll have to let that go---etc. Then continuing with a D which equals 500, and L equals 50, and sure enough, when we total it all up, excluding those letters that don't count anything--lo and behold, it equals 666. "I would like to submit that there are better interpretations for '666' than the Pope; in fact, I'd like to submit that anything bad or evil has been interpreted to be the number '666.' Someone didn't like Stalin, and so I have it run out here that Stalin equals 666, counting his name in Russian. The first cure of syphilis by Dr. Enlick was coded 666. When the Biblical scholars of the day saw what ridiculous arguments the Seventh-day Adventists were using, taking their theology to make history, which is a dangerous thing, they thought they would have some fun so they took the official name of the Seventh-day Adventist Church in the German language, coded it and out it came, 666. But some said, 'Well, you don't have a man's name; you don't have a person's name,' so they took the name of Helen Harmon White, who was the founder of the Seventh-day Adventists Church and coded her name. It comes out to be 666. I have Hitler, 666; Stalin, 666; the Pope, 666. You see, here's the point: John is talking about evil, he's talking about evilness, and he's pinpointing the evil in his own present time, which is, and I said it was clear as could be, the Emperor of Rome. He was doing all the persecutions. If we had the time I'd like to talk about 666 being the numerical equivalent of the name of the Emperor of Rome. He certainly is not talking about the Roman Catholic Church. "Another so called apostasy scriptures is in II Thessalonians, chapter 2:1-7. You know the day of the Lord will not come until there comes a falling away first and that man of sin be revealed, and the son of perdition who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called of God. Well, Paul is either talking about evil per se' or the Judaizers, or the Roman Empire, or perhaps even the Roman Emperor; but never is he talking about the Roman Catholic Church. "II Timothy, chapter 3:1-7.... This passage is counteracting Gnostics, especially the Libertine Gnostic groups who were active in the first two hundred years of the Christian era. The Libertines were certainly not part of the Roman Catholic Church. "How about I John 4:1-3, where he tells us to believe not every spirit, for there are many spirits

which have come from us and from without us, and they are the spirit of the anti-Christ because they teach that Jesus came not in the flesh. Anyone who knows any of the background of the Gnostic theologians in the early Christian Church knows what John is talking about. In both the Gospel of John and in the Epistles of John he is counteracting Gnosticism, not the Roman Catholic Church. "When rightly understood, all of these so-called Apostasy scriptures are dealing with apostasy, but not the apostasy directly involving the Roman Catholic Church. I am convinced that those who keep perpetuating this doctrine that the Roman Catholic Church is the Great and Abominable Church, have become so influenced by tradition, especially Protestant tradition which has been handed down for the many, many years, that they've failed to scrutinize and analyze the scriptures in their proper setting and in their proper light. I believe one fallacy has been to accuse the Roman Catholic Church as being the producers of the apostasy, and this is not so. That position is unfair to the Roman Catholic Church; she is not the producer of the apostasy. The Roman Catholic Church is what is left over after the apostasy. What produced the apostasy was very complex. It was a blending of many devilish churches and ideologies: the oriental and mystery religions, Judaism, the Judiazers, Pagan Rome, the Roman Imperial Priesthood, the cult of the hero worshipers, the cult of Emperor worship of the Roman Empire, and Gnosticism with all of its various brands and flavors. All of these together produced the apostasy. Therefore, the Roman Catholic Church is , in reality, the picking up of the pieces.... "In reality, the Great and Abominable Church is evilness. It is all that belongs to the Kingdom of Evil. . . . whether it be in Gnosticism, whether it be in the Roman Catholic Church, whether it be in the Mormon Church. I submit that there are people in the Latter-day Saint Church who are members of the Great and Abominable Church, as there are in other churches. "... What has been the greatest evil ever spoken against in very severe words from the pulpit by the President of the Church? Not the Roman Catholic Church, but other ideologies at work on this earth with the great masses of the people. He has characterized Communism as the greatest evil force existing on the earth today, organized as such. "So here, before Nephi's mind, the Lord had divided the world up into two great, broad categories of people; the one group composed the Church of the Lamb of God and the other was the Church of the devil, which must certainly be composed of evil, whether found in the Roman Catholic Church or Pagan Rome or in Babylonian religions or with the Pharaohs in the time of Egypt or with Gadianton and his brethren... "If we turn to section 18 of the Doctrine and Covenants, given before the Church was established, in verse 20 we read that the Lord told the missionaries that they were not to contend against any church, save it be the "church of the devil." The early Mormon missionaries did not go out and attack the Roman Catholic Church. In fact, the Prophet Joseph Smith wrote in his own documentary history, or his own historical journal, that the poor Catholics were not strong enough in America to even defend themselves, and, therefore we ought to uphold them. Never did Joseph Smith suggest that the Roman Catholic Church was the Great and Abominable Church; but, to the contrary, suggested to the Latter-day Saints that we uphold them. He asked that we strive to assure their religious freedom as we would the Methodists or the Presbyterians or our own. "In section 29 of the Doctrine and Covenants, verse 21, the Lord suggests that Ezekiel the Prophet talked about the Great and Abominable Church, even the destruction of the Great and Abominable Church, who was the whore of all the earth. When we read Ezekiel, it is clear that he certainly wasn't writing about the Roman Catholic Church. Ezekiel was writing about the great Northern princes of the heathen nations, Gog and Magog, because to Ezekiel these were the personification of all the evil that existed in Ezekiel's day. And he saw the downfall of evil; just like John saw the downfall of evil, just like Nephi saw the downfall of evil; not just the downfall of any particular church named the Roman Catholic Church or of any other church to be spelled out by name. "John Taylor, an apostle, wrote this article in the Times and Seasons in 1845: "'.....It will readily be perceived in the foregoing extract that John had no more reference to the Roman Catholic and Protestant churches who had a form of godliness, denying the power, than he had to all Babylon from Nimrod down. The old woman, Satan's wife, was drunken with the blood of the

2. She is characterized by the enjoyment of great wealth and luxury (17: 4; 18: 3, 11-16). 3. She (naturally is characterized by sexual immorality (17: 1, 2, 5). 4. She has dominion over all nations (17: 15, 18; 18: 3, 23-24). 5. Her fate is to be consumed by the very kings who have made war on the Lamb under the influence of her deceptions (17: 14-16; 18: 23). "It should be noted that one characteristic not common to both prophetic descriptions is Nephi's statement that the great and abominable church has held back important parts of the canon of scripture. But since John's record is one of the very scriptures Nephi refers to (14: 20-23), this omission in John's account is not surprising. "It must also be noted that in John's Revelation the whore cannot be equated with the two beasts; they do not represent the same things. The whore and the beasts are motivated by the same evil genius, Satan. The one beast supports the whore (Revelation 17: 3), but the beast and the whore are separate entities with separate functions in the evil empire. The whore of Revelation 17-18 is specifically the satanic counterpart of the woman in chapter 12 who symbolizes the Church of Jesus Christ which was forced into the wilderness (that is, she represents both sexual immorality and idolatry, the twin abominations of the Old Testament. It seems that in John's Revelation the symbol of the whore is used narrowly to represent false religion, while the beasts, the image of the beast, and the horns of the beast serve to represent other aspects of the kingdom of the devil. Moreover, if the symbol of the virtuous woman of Revelation 12 is intended to represent specifically the true Church of Jesus Christ (and the crown of Twelve Apostles and her being driven into the wilderness so indicate) it follows that the whore, her counterpart, represents specifically false and counterfeit religion. Satan has more than one institution at his disposal, but the whore is false religion. The whore cannot represent kingdoms or governments--the beast and its horns do that (Revelation 17: 12). But she can represent the false beliefs and ideologies that often capture or motivate governments. The whore provides the theory; government provides the muscle…. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . "It seems to me that many Latter-day Saints have made one of two errors in trying to identify the great and abominable church. The first is to believe that some specific denomination or other, to the exclusion of all others, has been the great and abominable church since the beginning of time. This is dangerous, for if we understand the great and abominable church to be one specific church, some will want to know which one it is, and an antagonistic relationship with that church or denomination will inevitably follow…. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . "…Often it has been suggested that the Roman Catholic Church might be the great and abominable church of 1 Nephi 13, but this is also untenable, primarily because Roman Catholicism as we know it did not yet exist when the crimes described by Nephi were being committed. In fact, the term Roman Catholic makes sense only after A.D. 1054, when it began to be used to distinguish the Western, Latin-speaking Orthodox church, which followed the bishop of Rome, from the Eastern, Greek-speaking Orthodox church, which followed the bishop of Constantinople (in association with others). Indeed, in the period between Peter and Constantine, there were other Christian churches besides the orthodox: Ebionites, Syrian and Egyptian Christians, Donatists, Gnostics, Marcionites, etc. We don't know very much about how they were related to each other. Even if we were to use the term 'Roman' Catholic for the church which Constantine began making his state religion in A.D. 313 (and the other orthodox churches would object to this), still the New Testament as we know it (that is, without the excised plain and precious parts) had already been widely circulated by then. In other words, the work of the great and abominable church in slaying the Apostles and excising the scriptures had already been done. By the time Constantine joined church and state together in the fourth century, the Apostles had been dead for centuries, and the true church and its keys had already been lost. The commonly held notion of shifty-eyed monks rewriting the scriptures as they copied is bigoted and unfair. In fact, we owe those monks a debt of gratitude that anything was saved at all. Besides, in comparison to some of the other Christian groups around, the orthodox Christians had quite a high standard of morality. By this time they

Stephen Robinson on the Great and Abominable Church: "While the revelation of John does not use the exact term great and abominable church, the entity so described by Nephi is clearly the harlot described by John in Revelation 17-18, since the identical terms mother of abominations, mother of harlots, and the whore who sitteth upon many waters are used by both prophets (see 1 Nephi 14: 10-12, 16 and Revelation 17: 1, 5). "Mayor characteristics of the great and abominable church in 1 Nephi may be listed as follows: 1. It persecutes, tortures, and slays the Saints of God (13: 5). 2. It seeks wealth and luxury (13: 7). 3. It is characterized by sexual immorality (13: 7). 4. It has excised plain and precious things from the scriptures (13: 26-29). 5. It has dominion over all the earth, among all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people (14: 11). 6. Its fate is to be consumed by a world war, in which the nations it incited against the Saints turn to war among themselves until they destroy the great and abominable church itself (22: 13-14). "These same characteristics are also attributed to the whore (Babylon) in the Revelation of John: 1. She is drunk with the blood of the Saints and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus and of the prophets (17: 6, 18: 24).

quotes.text: Saints and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus and the account actually included all whose "names were not written in the Book of Life from the foundation of the world." ...Besides the plainness of this scripture, other prophets have said many things of Babylon (and then he quotes from Jeremiah and ends up this way) Babylon, literally understood then, is the gay world, spiritual wickedness, the golden city and the glory of the world. The priests of Egypt who received a portion gratis from Pharaoh, the priests of Baal and the Pharisees and Saduccees with their long robes among the Jews are equally included in their mother's family with the Roman Catholic, Protestants and all that have not had the keys of the kingdom and the power thereof according to the ordinances of God. "B.H. Roberts in April Conference 1906 said this: "'The church of the devil here alluded to (he has been quoting Section 18 of the D&C) I understand to mean not any particular church among men or any one sect of religion, but something larger than that, something world-wide, something that includes within its boundaries all evil wherever it may be found, as well in schools of philosophy as in Christian sects, as well in systems of ethics as in systems of religion; something that includes the whole empire of Satan - what I shall call the Kingdom of Evil. This descriptive phrase, "The Church of the Devil," is also used in the Book of Mormon and while in attendance at a conference in one of the border stakes of Zion a question was propounded to me in relation to its meaning. The passage occurs in the writings of First Nephi, there are two churches only. And then the question came to him --Is the Catholic Church here referred to the Church of the Devil? Now, said I, in answer, I would not like to take that position because it would leave me with a lot of churches on my hands that I might not then be able to classify. So far as the Catholic Church is concerned I believe that there is just as much truth, nay, personally I believe that it has attained more truth than the other divisions of so-called Christendom and there is just as much virtue and I am sure there is more strength in the Roman Catholic Church than there is in Protestant Christendom. I would not like therefore to designate, the Roman Catholic Church as the Church of the Devil, neither would I like to designate any one of the various divisions and subdivisions of Protestant Christendom combined as such church. Nor the Greek Catholic Church nor the Buddhists nor the followers of Confuscius, nor the followers of Mohammed nor would I like to designate even the societies formed by deists or atheists as constituting the Church of the Devil. The Book of Mormon ought to be read in connection with its context with the chapter that precedes it and the remaining portion of the chapter in which it is found. Then I think those who study it in that manner would be forced to the conclusion then that the Prophet here has in mind no particular church, no particular division of Christendom, but he has in mind as just a whole empire of Satan. And perhaps the thought of the passage could be more nearly expressed if we used the term 'the kingdom of evil' as constituting 'the church of the devil.'
quotes.speaker: Reed C. Durham
quotes.attr: Reed C. Durham, "The Great and Abominable Church," Forum Address, Salt Lake Institute of Religion, Feb. 18, 1966

quotes.text: Bruce R.Mc Conkie on the Great and Abominable Church "As he viewed this day of darkness, this evil day after the apostolic era, he said: 'I saw among the nations of the Gentiles the foundation of a great church.' An angel with whom he was then conversing said: "Behold the foundation of a church which is most abominable above all other churches." Note it well: There are degrees of abomination; there are levels of iniquity; one church sinks deeper into the cesspool of sin than any other. The angels then described it as the church 'which slayeth the saints of God, yea, and tortureth them and bindeth them down, and yoketh them with a yoke of iron, and bringeth them down into captivity.' This is the kind of inspired utterance that is fulfilled over and over again by the same or an equivalent organization. As it happened in the first centuries of the Christian era, so, we may be assured, it has happened and will happen again in our dispensation. The day of persecution and martyrdom has not passed. "'And it came to pass that I beheld this great and abominable church,' Nephi said, 'and I saw the devil that he was the founder of it.' The man of sin is sitting in the temple of God, demanding worship, and proving thereby that he is God. 'And I also saw gold, and silver, and silks, and scarlets, and fine-twined linen, and all manner of precious clothing; and I saw many harlots,' Nephi continued. "Then from the angel came this word: 'Behold the gold, and the silver, and the silks, and the scarlets, and the fine-twined linen, and the precious clothing, and the harlots, are the desires of this great and abominable church. And also for the praise of the world do they destroy the saints of God, and bring them down into captivity.' (1 Nephi. 13:1-9) "John the Revelator saw the same vision that was given to Nephi. To John, an angel - perhaps the same one who conversed with Nephi - called the great and abominable church 'the great whore that sitteth upon many waters.' John saw her sitting 'upon a scarlet colored beast, full of names of blasphemy.... And the woman,' he said, 'was arrayed in purple and scarlet colour, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls, having a golden cup in her hand full of abominations and filthiness of her fornication: And upon her forehead was a name written, MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF HARLOTS AND ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH. And I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus.' (Rev. 17:1-6.) "This is the church of the devil that came into being after New Testament times. It was founded by Satan, and its interests lay in wealth and worldliness and carnality. This is the church into whose hands the scriptures came. These scriptures, so the angel said to Nephi, came 'from the Jews in purity unto the Gentiles.' As they were first written, they 'contained the plainness of the gospel of the Lord.' "The sad picture thus far portrayed is then softened somewhat by the angelic promise of a restoration of the gospel in the latter days; and the 'abominable church' is further identified as 'the mother of harlots,' which accords with what John wrote, and which can only mean that other churches spring from her. (1 Nephi 13:29-42.)
quotes.speaker: Bruce R. Mc Conkie
quotes.attr: Bruce R. Mc Conkie, The Millennial Messiah pp. 51-52, 53

quotes.text: Babylon is the name of the spirit that comes over those who kill the prophets. That spirit has infected much of the world. (See commentary on 16:6; also see 18:24; Matt. 23:34-35; 2 Cor. 4:8-11; Heb. 9:16.) The word drunken suggests that through the ages the righteous have been victims of a great slaughter-a slaughter that was intoxicating to the perpetrators.
quotes.source: Understanding the Book of Revelation [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co.
quotes.attr: Donald W. Parry and Jay A. Parry, Understanding the Book of Revelation [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co., 1998], 224

notes: Elder Mc Conkie states that the woman is "Rome, the capital city of the Babylonish Church." [MLM 440.] Others have suggested that the woman is "Roma, the goddess of Rome. She sits on Rome. The scarlet coloured beast, according to this explanation, is the whole Roman Empire. This is possibly born out by the suggestion that the "seven heads = the seven hills (Rome and seven emperors. One head 'was' (Nero), 'is not' (Nero was certainly dead), 'yet is' (Nero Redivivus)." [Reed C. Durham, "Revelations: The Plainest Book Ever Written," New Era, May 1973.] Stephen Robinson suggests that she is Hellenistic Christianity, which was tied very closely to the Roman Empire. [Stephen E. Robinson, Early Christianity and 1 Nephi 13-14," in The Book of Mormon: First Nephi, The Doctrinal Foundation, Second Annual Book of Mormon Symposium 1987, pp. 177-191.]

quotes.text: Robert Mounce explains how Rome could be the harlot by reminding that, "In the OT prophetic discourse the imagery of the harlot is commonly used to denote religious apostasy. Isaiah laments that the once faithful Jerusalem has become a harlot (Isa. 1: 21). Jeremiah speaks of Israel's 'adulteries and neighings' (elsewhere she is a wild ass in heat, Jer. 2: 24) and 'lewd harlotries' (Jer. 13: 27; cf. Jer. 2: 20-31; Ezek. 16: 15ff; Hos. 2: 5). Since the harlot of the Apocalypse is a pagan city (cf. 17: 18), it is more likely that a passage like Nahum 3: 4 or Isaiah 23: 16, 17 supplies the immediate background. In the former, the harlot is Nineveh, who betrays nations with her harlotries and her charms (cf. Rev. 17: 4). Isaiah pictures Tyre as a forgotten harlot. In the context of Revelation 17 and 18 the imagery is not that of religious profligacy but of the prostitution of all that is right and noble for the questionable ends of power and luxury. Whether Jezebel or Cleopatra sat for the portrait John is now painting makes little difference. The harlot is Rome. Adorned in luxury and intoxicated with the blood of the saints, she stands for a dominant world system based on seduction for personal gain over against the righteous demands of a persecuted minority. John's images are timeless in that they portray the essential conflicts of mankind from the beginning of time until the end. At the close of history the great harlot stands as the final and intensified expression of worldly power.
quotes.speaker: Robert Mounce
quotes.attr: Robert Mounce, "The Book of Revelation," The New Intrnl. Commentary, pp. 307-8

notes: This is obviously a parody of the Lamb of God, who was put to death yet came back to life and now is alive forevermore (Rev. 1: 18; 2: 8). It is also in opposition to the one "who is and who was and who is to come" (Rev. 1: 4, 8; 4: 8). In a broader sense, this is the beast of chapter 13 who, although having received the stroke of death, returns to hurl himself with renewed fury against the forces of God. It is this incredible power of resuscitation that causes those who dwell upon the earth to stand in awe." (Robert Mounce, "The Book of Revelation," The New Intrnl. Commentary, p. 312.) He is the same beast who had received a death-stroke in one of his heads and yet survived (13: 3, 12, 14). Though the beast itself is representative of kingdoms of this world (JST 13: 1), the inspiration of the beast is from the one who long ago promised to purchase kings, priests, etc. Throughout history, he repeatedly shows up in imitation of God. He claims to be the God of this world. When he is turned out, he returns. Even when he is bound, he will be loosed for one final short season.

quotes.text: The seven heads of the beast are first identified as seven mountains upon which the harlot is sitting. There is little doubt that a first-century reader would understand this reference in any way other than as a reference to Rome, the city built upon seven hills. Rome began as a network of seven hill settlements on the left bank of the Tiber, and was from the time of Servious Tullius (her sixth king)…
quotes.speaker: Robert Mounce
quotes.attr: Robert Mounce, "The Book of Revelation," The New Intrnl. Commentary, pp. 313-14

quotes.text: This seems to be a clear allusion to Rome, the city founded on seven hills, as the headquarters of that branch of Satan's kingdom which was persecuting and slaying the saints in John's day.
quotes.speaker: Bruce R. Mc Conkie
quotes.attr: Bruce R. Mc Conkie, DNTC 3: 554

notes: Vespasian A.D. 69 -- A.D. 79 Titus A.D. 79 -- A.D. 81 Domitian A.D. 81 -- A.D. 96 (Revelation is written) [Reed C. Durham, Revelations: The Plainest Book Ever Written, New Era, May 1973.] As indicated above, Durham sides with most biblical scholars in indicating that verses ten and eleven deal again (like chapter thirteen) with the Nero Redivivus Myth. Verse eleven is easier to fit with that concept than verse ten. Domitian, the emperor of John's time (maybe), is the eighth in line, (not allowing the three pretenders). Verse 10 is more difficult. Barclay presents a traditional scholarly view, with the admission that it is not a clean fit. "Five have fallen. The Roman Empire began with August; and the first five emperors were Augustus, Tiberius, Caligula, Claudius, and Nero. These, then, are the five who have fallen. We have already seen that after the death of Nero there were two years of chaos in which Galba, Otho and Vitellius followed each other in quick succession. They were not in any real sense emperors and cannot

I would add to Caird's point that the same pattern should be expected to be found in the seventh seal, else why go through all the hassle of understanding. Remember, John's record is about the future more than it is about his day.
Galba = 6 months Three military generals who acted as Otho = 8 months leaders over Rome for a short period. Vitelluus = 8 months

quotes.text: Verse ten is generally interpreted in the historical perspective of the Nero Redivivus concept. This may, in fact, be an accurate reflection of John's current sociopolitical scene. Keep in mind, however, that the real value in understanding the historical background of the scriptures is to shed light on how they impact on our day. John's vision, particularly, is of things in the future (seventh seal). Reed Durham is one or several Mormon scholars who uses the Nero Redivivus idea as an aid to understand some of what John is saying. The following list of emperors is taken form his writings. In verse eleven of chapter seventeen, Durham interprets the: One head that 'was' as Nero, the head that 'is not' means that Nero was certainly dead, and yet ' is' as Nero Redivivus. Augustus 27 B.C. -- A.D. 14 (Christ Born) Tiberius A.D. 14 -- A.D. 37 (Christ's ministry and death) Caligula A.D. 37 -- A.D. 41 Claudius A.D. 41 -- A.D. 54 Nero A.D. 54 -- A.D. 68
quotes.speaker: Nero Redivivus myth
quotes.attr: Nero Redivivus myth

notes: Remember that the Prophet Joseph indicated that the beasts were in the likeness of kingdoms. (JST 13: 1.) Mounce, if he is right, seems to suggest that verse eleven pinpoints a symbolic personification of the beast. For John's time, this could very well have been a Nero Redivivus. An example of evil personified in our time would be Hitler. Often, tyrants are measured by his standards of evil.

quotes.text: One speculative interpretation is offered by the Protestant scholar, Robert Mounce. Just as Babylon is a symbol of sin (DNTC 3: 559), this beast which is personified by being the eighth head,"John now arrives at the heart of the riddle. The beast himself is an eighth king who is at the same time one of the seven. If the seven kings were specific Roman emperors, then the most likely candidate for number eight would be Domitian. This would identify the beast as the emperor reigning at the time John receives his vision. Yet three times in the chapter we are told that the beast is not (17: 8 [twice], 11). At the time of writing, the beast has not yet ascended from the abyss (17: 8). He is an eighth in the sense that he is distinct from the other seven. He is Antichrist, not simply another Roman emperor. He is not a human ruler through whom the power of evil finds expression--he is that evil power itself. He belongs to the cosmic struggle between God and Satan which lies behind the scenes of human history. Ye he will appear on the stage of history as a man. He is of the seven--not one of the seven--in that he plays the same sort of role as his earthly predecessors. He himself, however, belongs to another sphere of reality. His period of hegemony is the greater tribulation preceding the return of the Messiah. This interpretation requires no reliance upon the Nero Redivivus myth, although its existence would aid in the understanding of John's prophecy of Antichrist. Nero, the epitome of evil and the abuse of power, was (he lived and ruled) and is not (that is, he is now dead). Yet (according to the myth) he will return to life and once again seize power. Commentators who understand the eighth king as Domitian stress that early writers recognized a resemblance between Nero and Domitian. But since all the beast's heads were part of the same imperial system through which the evil intent of Antichrist found expression, we would of course expect the two prominent persecutors to be comparable.
quotes.speaker: Robert Mounce
quotes.attr: Robert Mounce, "The Book of Revelation," The New Intrnl. Commentary, p. 316

notes: This is an excellent insight in that it reminds us that though John's vision uses typology and metaphors from his day, it is a vision of "that which is to come."

quotes.text: One author identifies these ten kings as "purely eschatological figures representing the totality of the powers of all nations on the earth which are to be made subservient" to Satan. Recall that the number ten is symbolic and indicates completeness. It does not point to ten specific kings nor to ten European kingdoms of a revived Roman empire.
quotes.speaker: I. T. Beckwith
quotes.attr: I. T. Beckwith, The Apocalypse of John, p. 700

notes: It would be an error to blame some modern denomination for the activities of an ancient great and abominable church. The other error is to go too far the other way, dehistoricizing the abominable church altogether. The term then becomes merely a vague symbol for all the disassociated evil in the world. We cannot, in the face of the scriptural evidence, accept this view. For if we do, we shall not be able to recognize the categories and know who is playing the role of Babylon in our own times or in times to come. Thus, we must, on the one hand, avoid the temptation to identify the role of the great and abominable church so completely with one particular entity that we do not recognize the part when it is played by some other entity. At the same time, we must remember that the role will be played by some entity or coalition, and we must be able to tell by their characteristic fruits which is Zion and which is Babylon.

By the time of Constantine, the Apostles had been dead for centuries. Furthermore, the early Orthodox church can hardly be accused of immorality. It had, in fact, gone to the extremes of asceticism. In some areas of the world Orthodoxy replaced an earlier, already corrupt form of Christianity. And during much of the period, members of the Orthodox Church were not in a position to persecute anyone, as they were being thrown to the lions themselves. The Catholic church of the fourth century was the result of the Apostasy—its end product—not the cause. To find the real culprits, we need to look at a much earlier period in church history than the fourth century after Christ. Satan had his ministers in the world long before then, and we must remember that Babylon was already there to oppose Zion in the days of Cain, Nimrod, Pharaoh, and Herod.

Perhaps the greatest difficulty in understanding Nephi’s description of the great and abominable church is what seems to be a contradiction between chapter 13 and chapter 14. In 1 Nephi 13, [1 Ne. 13, 1 Ne. 14] the great and abominable church is one specific church among many. Nephi’s description of it as “most abominable above all other churches” (1 Ne. 13:5, 26) does not make sense otherwise. Moreover, the great and abominable church in chapter 13 has a specific historical description: it was formed among the Gentiles after the Jews transmitted the Bible in its purity to the Gentiles. (1 Ne. 13:26.) It is also the specific historical agent responsible for excising plain and precious truths from the scriptural record.

The war with Babylon, the great and abominable, began before the world was formed and continues through all ages of time. In 1 Nephi 13–14, [1 Ne. 13–14] the prophet Nephi relates a vision in which he saw the future of the world and its kingdoms as it related to his posterity. Nephi’s vision is the type of revelation known in biblical literature as apocalyptic, a type represented in the New Testament most fully by the Revelation of John. The two revelations have more in common, though, than apocalyptic form, for they both deal in part with an often misunderstood concept, the great and abominable church of the devil. The visions together give us prophetic information about the matter.

quotes.text: Babylon in the first and second centuries may even have been a collection of different movements. Some Jewish Christians couldn’t let go of the law of Moses and eventually gave up Christ instead. The Orthodox Christians adopted Greek philosophy. The Gnostics wallowed in the mysteries and in unspeakable practices on the one hand or in neurotic asceticism on the other. Second-century compilers like Tatian and Marcion rewrote the scriptures, the latter boldly chopping out anything he didn’t like. And all of them together forced the virtuous woman, the true church of Jesus Christ, into the wilderness.
quotes.speaker: Stephen E. Robinson
quotes.attr: Stephen E. Robinson, “Warring against the Saints of God,” Ensign, Jan 1988, 34

quotes.text: Premortality is not a relaxing doctrine. For each of us, there are choices to be made, incessant and difficult chores to be done, ironies and adversities to be experienced, time to be well spent, talents and gifts to be well employed. Just because we were chosen “there and then,” surely does not mean we can be indifferent “here and now.” Whether foreordination for men, or foredesignation for women, those called and prepared must also prove “chosen, and faithful” (See Revelation 17:14; D&C 121:34–36.)
quotes.speaker: Neal A. Maxwell
quotes.attr: Neal A. Maxwell, in Conference Report, Oct. 1985, 21; or Ensign, Nov. 1985, 17

quotes.text: We find that after the days of Noah an order was introduced called the patriarchal order, in which every man managed his own family affairs. And prominent men among them were kings and priests unto God, and officiated in what is known among us as the priesthood of the Son of God, or the priesthood after the order of Melchizedek. Man began again to multiply on the face of the earth, and the heads of families became their kings and priests, that is, the fathers of their own people. And they were more or less under the influence and guidance of the Almighty. We read, for instance, in our revelations pertaining to these matters, of a man called Melchizedek, who was a great high priest. We are told that there were a great many high priests in his day, and before him and after him; and these men had communication with God, and were taught of him in relation to their general proceedings, and acknowledged the hand of God in all things which they were associated.
quotes.speaker: John Taylor
quotes.attr: John Taylor, The Gospel Kingdom, 139

quotes.text: Another symbol used in the book of Revelation to represent the great and abominable church, as well as worldliness and wickedness in general, is Babylon. Five of the six characteristics identified in 1 Nephi are also attributed to Babylon in the book of Revelation: 1. Babylon is drunk with the blood of the Saints, the martyrs of Jesus, and the prophets. (See Rev. 17:6; Rev. 18:24.) 2. She is known for her enjoyment of great wealth and luxury. (See Rev. 17:4; Rev. 18:3, 11–16.) 3. She is characterized by wanton sexual immorality. (See Rev. 17:1–2, 5.) 4. She has dominion over all nations. (See Rev. 17:15, 18; Rev. 18:3, 23–24.) 5. Her fate is to be consumed by the very kings who, because of her deceptions, have made war on the Lamb.

In this sense, the church of the devil is equivalent to that great and spacious building seen in vision by both Nephi and his father. (See 1 Ne. 8:26–28, 31, 33–34; 1 Ne. 11:35–36.) The apocalyptic description of the great and spacious building matches the characteristics of the church of the devil; the artificial structure without foundation represents the carnal world, and its values and life-style include mockery of the kingdom of God. It fights against the Apostles of Jesus Christ, and its fall will be great, for “thus shall be the destruction of all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people, that shall fight against the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

When we put all this together, we find that the term great and abominable church means an immense assembly or association of people bound together by their loyalty to that which God hates. Most likely, this “church” is involved specifically in sexual immorality, idolatry (that is, false worship), or both. While the book of Revelation does not use the exact phrase “great and abominable church,” both John and Nephi use a number of similar phrases to describe it. They call it the “Mother of Harlots, and Abominations,” “mother of abominations,” and “the whore that sitteth upon many waters.

The church of the devil is every evil and worldly organization on earth. It is all of the systems, both Christian and non-Christian, that have perverted the pure and perfect gospel; it is all of the governments and powers that run counter to the divine will; it is the societies and political parties and labor unions that sow strife and reap contention. It is communism; it is Islam; it is Buddhism; it is modern Christianity in all its parts. It is Germany under Hitler, Russia under Stalin, and Italy under Mussolini. It is the man of sin speaking in churches, orating in legislative halls, and commanding the armies of men. And its headquarters are everywhere--in Rome and Moscow, in Paris and London, in Teheran and Washington--everywhere that evil forces, either of church or state or society, can be influenced. The immanent and all-pervading presence of evil in high places is one of the signs of the times.
quotes.speaker: Bruce R. Mc Conkie
quotes.attr: Bruce R. Mc Conkie, The Millennial Messiah pp. 54-55

notes: Cross reference with verse 12. Notice that these ten horns are unlike the ten horns of chapter thirteen's

quotes.text: It is quite clear in this verse that the Lord can use all, righteous or wicked, in bringing about his eternal purposes.
quotes.attr: See also Mormon 4:5 and Moses 4:6

notes: "Again this seems to be a clear allusion to Rome, the capital city and center of civil autocracy, social degeneracy, and spiritual disease and death. 'The state of society at Rome, at the time of the Revelation,' Dummelow says, 'was probably the worst the world had ever seen.' This conclusion is, of course, an exaggeration since the most degenerate social circumstances were those of Noah's day. But Dummelow's description is apt in pointing up the difficulties facing the meridian saints. He continues: 'The aristocracy, which alone had any voice in public affairs, was, with few exceptions, utterly given over to the most shameless wickedness. Vast wealth was in their hands, which was spent in unbridled luxury and debauchery. Their continual craving for new sensations was ministered to by foreign parasites, who introduced new vices and flagrant superstitions. With no feeling for others, their cruelty was appalling. With their appetite for life jaded by the pursuit of pleasure, suicide became common. The herded masses of the people were sunk in ignorance and pauperism. The public distribution of corn confirmed them in idleness, and the public shows helped to harden their hearts and to corrupt their feelings. The state religion was not believed in by the educated, while it had no moral teaching to provide for those who did believe in it, and there was no system of public education.' (Dummelow, p. 1086.)" [Bruce R. Mc Conkie, DNTC 3: 555-6.]

quotes.text: The Babylonian captivity was perhaps the lowest point of Israel's history. Elder Bruce R. Mc Conkie wrote: "Both Isaiah and Jeremiah spoke at length of the devastations of that day and of the cursings and destruction that would come upon Babylon as a result. (Isa. 13:1 and 21; Jer. 50:1 and 51.) Babylon was truly the great enemy of the Lord's people anciently, and her overthrow and the destruction of her worldliness and wickedness was one of the things of greatest interest and concern to them. What was more natural, then, than for John and all the prophets to use Babylon as the symbol of sin, and her destruction as the overthrow of wickedness on earth.
quotes.speaker: Bruce R. Mc Conkie
quotes.attr: Bruce R. Mc Conkie, Doctrinal New Testament Commentary, 3:559

quotes.text: Revelation 18 A. Introduction (Babylon's Doom Pronounced; Her Internal Condition Described: Her Sinful Activities Summarized) 1-3 B. "Interlude" (Appeal to "Come Out of Babylon"; Statement of Judgment on Babylon), 4-8 C. The Litany Proper (Mourning of Kings, Merchants, and Seafarers at Babylon in Flames), 9-19 B'. "Interlude" (Call for Rejoicing; Statement of Judgment on Babylon), 20 A'. Conclusion (Babylon's Doom Graphically Expressed; Her Internal Condition Described; Her Sinful Activities and Relationships Summarized). 21-24
quotes.speaker: Kenneth A. Strand
quotes.attr: Kenneth A. Strand, Modalities of Symbolic Usage, Andrews University Seminary Studies, Spring 1986, 24:38
